..summary: AuthCodeStore.createAuthCode documents @throws IllegalArgumentException for null/blank jwt but implementations don't validate

evidence: AuthCodeStore interface line 10: /** @throws IllegalArgumentException if jwt is null or blank */
RedisAuthCodeStore.createAuthCode (line 60-63) stores jwt directly via redisTemplate without null/blank check
InMemoryAuthCodeStore.createAuthCode (TestAuthCodeStoreConfig line 36-40) also stores jwt without validation
detail.suggestion: Add validation at the start of both createAuthCode implementations: if (jwt == null || jwt.isBlank()) throw new IllegalArgumentException("jwt must not be null or blank"); — or add a default method in the interface itself to centralize the precondition check.

..summary: @Autowired used on 4 single-constructor classes while 11+ omit it, creating inconsistent DI annotation style

evidence: GoogleTokenVerifier line 27: @Autowired on single-constructor class
MicrosoftTokenVerifier line 20: @Autowired on single-constructor class
JwtTokenService line 36: @Autowired on single-constructor class
RedisAuthCodeStore line 49: @Autowired on single-constructor class

AuthCompletionService, UserService, ProviderConfigController, UserController, OAuth2SuccessHandler, IdentityContractGuard, JwtAuthenticationFilter, MicrosoftAuthorizationGateFilter, RedisMicrosoftChallengeStore, SecurityConfig, ProviderIdentityNormalizer — all omit @Autowired on single constructors
detail.suggestion: Remove @Autowired from the 4 annotated single-constructor classes to match the majority pattern. Spring 4.3+ implicit injection makes them unnecessary.

..summary: Config-layer classes import ErrorResponse from controller.dto, creating a downward dependency

evidence: SecurityConfig.java:5 imports com.demo.sso.controller.dto.ErrorResponse — config depends on controller
MicrosoftAuthorizationGateFilter.java:4 imports com.demo.sso.controller.dto.ErrorResponse — config.filter depends on controller.dto
ErrorResponse is a trivial record (1 field) used only for JSON serialization in handleUnauthorized() and the gate filter's 503 response
detail.suggestion: Move ErrorResponse to a shared package (e.g., com.demo.sso.dto or com.demo.sso.model.dto) accessible to both config and controller layers, or inline the JSON construction in the two config-layer usages: response.getWriter().write(objectMapper.writeValueAsString(Map.of("error", message)))

..summary: AuthCodeStore.createAuthCode documents @throws IllegalArgumentException for null/blank jwt but RedisAuthCodeStore doesn't enforce it

evidence: AuthCodeStore line 10: javadoc '@throws IllegalArgumentException if jwt is null or blank'
RedisAuthCodeStore.createAuthCode lines 60-63: generates code and writes to Redis without any null/blank validation of the jwt parameter
detail.suggestion: Add a guard at the top of RedisAuthCodeStore.createAuthCode: if (jwt == null || jwt.isBlank()) throw new IllegalArgumentException("jwt must not be null or blank"). Alternatively, remove the @throws from the interface if validation is not intended.

..summary: User.getGoogleId() and setGoogleId() lack @Deprecated annotation despite field being deprecated

evidence: User.java line 54: @Deprecated on googleId field, but lines 92-93: getGoogleId()/setGoogleId() have no @Deprecated
In Java, field-level @Deprecated does not propagate to accessor methods — callers using user.getGoogleId() get no compiler warning
UserService.createNewUser() line 74 calls user.setGoogleId() without any deprecation warning; JwtTokenService.generateToken() line 107 calls user.getGoogleId() without warning
detail.suggestion: Add @Deprecated annotation to getGoogleId() and setGoogleId() methods to ensure callers receive compiler deprecation warnings during the migration period

..summary: useAuth returns error as string|null while useProviderConfig returns Error|null — inconsistent error types across hooks

evidence: useAuth (line 26): useState<string | null>(null) — error state is string|null
useProviderConfig (line 14): useState<Error | null>(null) — error state is Error|null
Consumers must check error type differently depending on which hook they use
detail.suggestion: Align both hooks to return the same error type. Recommend Error|null for both, since useProviderConfig already wraps non-Error values. In useAuth, wrap string errors: setError(new Error(message)) and update the type to Error|null.

..summary: CSRF exempt paths in SecurityConfig are maintained as separate constants from controller @RequestMapping — parallel maintenance risk

evidence: SecurityConfig lines 25-28 define AUTH_GOOGLE_VERIFY_PATH, AUTH_MICROSOFT_PATH, AUTH_EXCHANGE_PATH, AUTH_LOGOUT_PATH as private constants
AuthController uses @PostMapping annotations with path fragments ('/google/verify', '/microsoft/challenge', etc.) under @RequestMapping('/auth')
If a controller path changes without updating SecurityConfig's CSRF exemptions, CSRF protection would silently re-engage on auth POST endpoints, breaking them
detail.suggestion: Extract shared path constants into AuthPaths class or use a wildcard CSRF exemption for /auth/** (since all /auth endpoints are already permitAll and stateless JWT-based, CSRF protection is unnecessary for the entire /auth namespace). The wildcard approach is simpler and eliminates the parallel maintenance problem entirely.

..summary: MicrosoftTokenVerifier.verifyIdToken lacks @throws documentation despite throwing InvalidTokenException and propagating JwtException

evidence: MicrosoftTokenVerifier.verifyIdToken (line 30) has no Javadoc at all
Method throws InvalidTokenException from validateAudience, validateIssuer, validateVersion, validateNonce (lines 40-66)
Method propagates JwtException from jwtDecoder.decode(credential) on line 31
Caller AuthController.verifyMicrosoftToken correctly catches both InvalidTokenException and JwtException (line 156), indicating awareness of the undocumented contract
detail.suggestion: Add Javadoc to verifyIdToken documenting: @throws InvalidTokenException if audience, issuer, version, or nonce validation fails; @throws org.springframework.security.oauth2.jwt.JwtException if the token cannot be decoded or signature validation fails

..summary: OAuth2SuccessHandler throws InvalidTokenException and IllegalArgumentException that escape the try-catch boundary, bypassing the redirect error pattern

evidence: Line 64: throw new IllegalArgumentException('Expected OAuth2AuthenticationToken') — thrown inside authenticateAndCreateCode but not caught by line 50's catch(OAuth2IdentityException)
Line 90: throw new InvalidTokenException('Unsupported provider: ' + registrationId) — also escapes the OAuth2IdentityException catch block

Lines 50-53: only OAuth2IdentityException is caught, redirecting with error code — other exceptions propagate to Spring Security's error handling, likely producing a 500 page instead of a user-friendly redirect
detail.suggestion: Wrap the unsupported-provider and unexpected-auth-type cases in OAuth2IdentityException so they follow the same redirect-with-error-code pattern as identity validation failures. E.g., throw new OAuth2IdentityException("unsupported_provider", "Unsupported provider: " + registrationId)

..summary: buildMicrosoftConfig packs 6 ternary-per-field arguments into one constructor call

evidence: Lines 83-89: new MicrosoftProviderConfig(serverSideEnabled, clientSideEnabled, clientSideEnabled ? ... : null, clientSideEnabled ? ... : null, clientSideEnabled ? ... : List.of(), clientSideEnabled ? ... : null) — 4 of 6 args repeat the same ternary guard on clientSideEnabled
detail.suggestion: Extract the clientSideEnabled check once and use local variables for the conditional fields, or add a static factory MicrosoftProviderConfig.clientSideDisabled(serverSideEnabled) to eliminate the repeated ternary pattern.

1 boilerplate_duplication work items — extract shared boilerplate into reusable helpers or base classes
15 review work items need investigation — run `desloppify show review --status open` to see them
37 stale_wontfix work items — re-evaluate old wontfix decisions — fix, document, or escalate
2.5 pts of wontfix debt — review stale decisions
